title: Bridge Essay: Brain-based predictive modeling of mind-wandering and involuntary thought: the idiographic approach.
concept: The natural mind — spontaneous thought as baseline state, fabrication as deliberate override.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** The natural mind — spontaneous thought as baseline state, fabrication as deliberate override.

**Brain-based predictive modeling of mind-wandering and involuntary thought: the idiographic approach.** - Technology, mind, and behavior (2025). DOI: 10.1037/tmb0000155. PMID: 41050630. A substantial portion of waking life consists of mental experiences that arise involuntarily, including mind-wandering, spontaneous thought and rumination. Given that these experiences are fundamental to cognitive function and mental health, there has been growing scientific and clinical interest in using measures of brain activity to model and predict the momentary occurrence of these various forms of involuntary thought.
